grammar.title: Co-sender ‘y el hermano Timoteo’
grammar.detail: Like Philippians, Paul adds a co-sender. ‘el hermano Timoteo’ — definite article before the noun and the personal name afterwards: very common Spanish order.

expression.phrase: apóstol de Cristo Jesús por la voluntad de Dios
expression.note: Paul’s standard opening — identical wording to Ephesians and 2 Corinthians. Authority grounded in God’s will, not human appointment.

grammar.title: ‘Que… les conceda’
grammar.detail: Same blessing formula as Eph 1:2 and Phil 1:2, but singular this time: ‘Dios nuestro Padre… conceda’. ‘conceda’ = 3rd singular present subjunctive.

grammar.title: ‘los que están en Colosas’
grammar.detail: Relative clause with ‘que’ + locative. Identifies the audience by where they live — common in NT letter openings.

expression.phrase: santos y fieles hermanos en Cristo
expression.note: Three descriptors stacked: holy, faithful, brothers — all qualified by ‘en Cristo’. Paul’s shorthand for ‘authentic Christians’.

grammar.title: ‘siempre que’ + indicative
grammar.detail: ‘whenever’ with a habitual present — indicative: ‘siempre que oramos’. (Future / hypothetical contexts would take subjunctive.)

expression.phrase: el Padre de nuestro Señor Jesucristo
expression.note: A Pauline title for God. By naming God this way, Paul places the gospel within the Father-Son relationship — Jesus is no afterthought.
